ip_outcome_note: Card name is 'Klang'; the depicted figure is canonically Klang (National Pokedex no. 600, Steel-type Stage 1 in the Klink line). Verified via pokemondb.net and card name match — depicted gear figure with face-like center on large gear matches Klang's canonical design. Flavor text from PokemonTCG.io also confirms species. ip_verified set to true, ip_confidence remains high (no downgrade needed). Internal Pokemon IP — no IP callout block required.

trivia_bullets.text: Klang is National Pokedex no. 600, the Stage 1 mid-form of the Klink evolution line: Klink (no. 599) -> Klang (no. 600, evolves at level 38) -> Klinklang (no. 601, evolves at level 49). Introduced in Generation 5 (Unova) in Pokemon Black and White (2010/2011). The Klink line was one of the first Pokemon designed as purely mechanical entities rather than animals adapted with metallic features. [pokemondb.net: Klang Pokedex page; Bulbapedia: Klang, via WebSearch snippet]

Flavor text: 'When Klang goes all out, the minigear links up perfectly with the outer part of the big gear, and this Pokemon's rotation speed increases sharply.' [pokemon.com TCG database: Darkness Ablaze 126]. This mechanically describes the same gear-linkage visible in the card art — the smaller minigear aligned with the larger gear's outer ring, conveying mechanical efficiency as a game state escalation.

Darkness Ablaze no. 126/189, Stage 1, Steel-type, 90 HP, Uncommon, illustrated by NC Empire, released 2020-08-14. NC Empire is a studio illustrator with a large catalog on Serebii's artist index; credited on multiple Steel-type and mechanical-Pokemon cards across SWSH era. The Darkness Ablaze set (SWSH3) is themed around Charizard VMAX and includes Eternatus VMAX as its second marquee card; Klang is a filler uncommon in the Steel-type slot. [Scryfall-equivalent: pokemon.com TCG database; Serebii: NC Empire artist page, via WebSearch snippet]
sources: Serebii: NC Empire artist index, via WebSearch snippet
